programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony's one-time Baltimore PD partner Danny Price is murdered in a manner that suggests the port-to-port killer has struck again. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell, LL Cool J, Daniela Ruah and Eric Christian Olsen(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs travels to his home town after hearing news of his father's death, while the team investigates whether a fire on a US Navy ship was accidental or planned. The vessel served as prison to several members of the Brotherhood of Doubt terrorist group, whom Gibbs was responsible for catching. After the blaze, two of those members are missing. Tony argues that Gibbs would want to know, but Vance does not want him disturbed at this difficult time. Mark Harmon stars(n)

Crime drama, starring Daniela Ruah, Eric Christian Olsen and Chris O'Donnell(n)

programme.desc: Gibbs discovers a link between the murder of a petty officer and the break-up of his team, leading him and new director Leon Vance to uncover a case of blackmail involving an NCIS agent. However, Bristol depot manager Mike battles with traffic to deliver enough products for the holiday-makers, while in Penzance the fridges break down on one of the hottest days of the year. programme.title: Elton John: Uncensored
programme.desc: As he prepares for the publication of his autobiography, Me, and enjoys a break from his Farewell Yellow Brick Road tour, Elton John and Graham Norton chat about the musician's life and career, which spans more than 50 years. Elton discusses his childhood growing up as Reg Dwight in post-war Pinner, Middlesex, and looks back at his rise to global stardom in the 1970s. He also shares musical memories of the 80s and 90s, discusses his addictions, and how he managed to overcome them to become one of the world's most successful and prolific solo artists(n)

Chantelle lays into Mitch about his parenting when he fails to return home after a date(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville creates a special blend of snuff, but will his customers notice any beneficial effects? The ladies get dressed up for line-dancing, while Gastric is prompted to go on a diet. A furtive Mr Newbold thinks he's found a pastime to share with Mrs Rossi - away from the prying eyes of Mrs Featherstone. However, a determined Black Widow enlists assistance from Granville and Leroy for a crash-course in how to ride a bicycle. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Peaky Blinders star Helen McCrory assumes the role of host, as contributors poke fun at the week's events. Stand-up comedian Chris McCausland is one of the panellists joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Guests include US politician Hillary Clinton and her daughter Chelsea, co-authors of The Book of Gutsy Women. Superstar chef and campaigner Jamie Oliver promotes his latest cookbook Veg, while comedian and actor David Mitchell talks about his collection of satirical columns, Dishonesty Is the Second-Best Policy. Plus, Kesha performs her current single Raising Hell(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: A counselling session elicits an uncomfortable truth from Fleabag and she finds herself somewhere unexpected. Meanwhile, a chat with her sister brings some unwelcome news, Fleabag faces off with her brother-in-law Martin, and Jake wonders where Claire is. Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge, with Sian Clifford and Brett Gelman(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates travel to the world-famous BBC Maida Vale Studios in West London, where Lord Sugar informs them that for their latest challenge, they are to become managers for musicians. The teams must audition unsigned artists before choosing which one to represent. They will then head to the studio to create a brand-new remix of one of their tracks, aiming to secure sales of the record at their own showcase events. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants is sent home from the competition(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the mysterious murder of a Yorkshire farm owner in 1933, finding out if a clandestine love affair may have led to a brutal killing(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a wooden desk, a scrapbook charting the fantastic story of the owner's grandmother, and a wind up train that has not run in decades(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Helen and Rob live in a Victorian terraced house in St Albans. They have put all their energy into renovating the entrance and the front room, but the back of the house remains a mishmash of tiny and unpractical rooms. Architects Robert Jamison and Laura Jane Clark compete to take control of this renovation project. Angela Scanlon presents(n)

programme.title: Darcey Bussell: Looking for Fred Astaire
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing judge and former ballet star Darcey Bussell always wishes she could have danced with Fred Astaire, who died 30 years ago this year. Now she finds out all about him, on a journey that takes her from Nebraska to New York and from London to Los Angeles. Darcey discovers how Fred only started dancing to help his sister Adele, she learns about his discipline and inventiveness, and of course his Hollywood partnership with Ginger Rogers(n)

programme.title: Daddy Long Legs (1955)
programme.desc: An 18-year-old Frenchwoman falls in love with an older man, unaware he is the secret millionaire benefactor who saved her from a Paris orphanage and sponsored her education in America. Romantic musical, with Fred Astaire, Leslie Caron and Fred Clark(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball presents more backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing. Plus, Ian Waite casts his eye over the couples' latest training footage(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Loose Women panellists Andrea McLean and Penny Lancaster search the antiques shops of Essex in the hope of making a profit, with expert guidance from auctioneer Tim Medhust and dealer David Harper. Penny is already a keen collector and even finds the time to pick up a gift for husband Rod Stewart. How will newbie Andrea compare against her more experienced colleague?(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the last heat of the competition, the first three chefs recreate Monica's dish of oeufs meurette, before the next three take on Marcus' drop scones and a suzette sauce. In the next round, the six chefs are given a chance to showcase their creativity and cooking style as they present a dish of their own design to the judges(n)

programme.title: The Baby Has Landed
programme.desc: Documentary series following families throughout their first weeks of parenthood, lifting the lid on one of the most joyful, yet intense, times in a family's life. The opening episode introduces the first set of the six expectant families featured in the series, including young first-time parents Syler and Mo Nicholson-Bayoumi from Nottinghamshire, and Helen and Nigel Pierce, who are expecting their fourth child(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: The companion programme to The Apprentice, with Tom Allen talking to the ninth candidate to be ejected from the competition. The host is also joined by a panel of experts and fans, who offer an entertaining analysis of this week's task(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The vitamin industry is worth more than £400million a year in the UK alone, but what are these pills actually doing for people's health? Dr Giles Yeo aims to find out, exploring if vitamin and mineral supplements are what they appear to be and putting people's diets to the test to find out if they really need these supplements. Those he meets include a scientist in Copenhagen, whose groundbreaking research into antioxidants indicates they may not have the extraordinary health-enhancing properties that people believe, and a nutrition scientist from the University of Greenwich, who tells Giles that on average most people in the UK are meeting their vitamin requirements thorough their diet(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of Sainsbury's. After a challenging Christmas, the staff go all out to maximise profits with their flower sales over the Mother's Day weekend. Plans are also made to sell a birthday cake to celebrate the company's 150th year of trading, bringing in Claire Ptak, the creator of Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding cake, for ideas. Plus, last year CEO Mike Coupe announced a possible merger with Asda. Will industry regulators give the company the go-ahead to become the biggest supermarket in the country?(n)

programme.title: Novels That Shaped Our World
programme.desc: Documentary examining the impact of novels from three perspectives - the empire, class experience and, in this first film, women's voices. The programme aims to show the plight of women is a theme that reaches right back to the earliest novels, with extracts from Jane Austen, the Brontes, Mary Shelley and Virginia Woolf, through to the post-war publishing boom where a new generation of global writers such as Zadie Smith, Toni Morrison, Arundhati Roy and Margaret Atwood have continued to speak out for women to a new generation of readers(n)

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the structure in its 25th anniversary year, following the teams who keep everything on track through the undersea tunnel. Workers in the tunnel paint two giant murals that will link to each other across the sea, and the catenary team head out in their van to look for repairs. Inside the tunnel, the high-speed trains are being slowed down by the air they push in front of them, and the only way to relieve the pressure is with tubes connecting one tunnel to another(n)

programme.title: The A List
programme.desc: Amber lands her cruellest blow yet on Mia and celebrates in her cabin with a select few, but things soon spin out of control. Alex and Petal grow closer(n)

programme.title: The A List
programme.desc: Dev makes a timely discovery that leads him to ask dangerous questions, while Mia, Alex and Harry unearth a potential way to stop Amber - if they can figure out what it means(n)

programme.title: The A List
programme.desc: Mia, Alex and Harry must decide whether or not to trust a potential new ally who could provide them with vital information. Kayleigh learns the true cost of Amber's favour(n)

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine the case of a poultry farmer who was hanged for the murder of his wife, after her body was found buried under a chicken run(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life, including a collectable seesaw, a hand-stitched bedspread and a gentlemen's shooting stick(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: This time, Nick Knowles and the team transforms a home in Bromsgrove. Karis, a 28-year-old woman who has cerebral palsy, currently lives with her mother, Karen, but now her own health is deteriorating and it is making caring for Karis almost impossible. The family have decided that Karis should move in with her older sister Jo, but her entire home is inaccessible for Karis, so the team decides to build a large two-storey extension to make the house more suitable. Elsewhere, Billy takes on jousting at Warwick Castle(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Eggheads

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott chooses four dishes that really push things to the limit, each providing a technical challenge that could inspire viewers to tackle something new. The menu includes the Hairy Bikers' sushi, James Martin's crown of lamb, Raymond Blanc with his lobster with red pepper and cardamom jus, and the ultimate chocolate cake from Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood(n)

programme.title: Darcey Bussell: Dancing to Happiness
programme.desc: The ex-Strictly judge meets people who use dance as therapy to see if it works, as part of her mission to improve the state of the nation's mental health. In Manchester, Darcey meets inspiring choreographer and dancer Kevin Turner, who has started a therapeutic dance project for young women who have been referred by a local support group, while in Bury, the Silver Swans are using dance to overcome the isolation and loneliness that so often troubles people when they retire(n)

programme.title: Call Me Madam (1953)
programme.desc: A Washington socialite becomes ambassador to a small European state and falls in love with the foreign minister, while her sidekick attracts the attention of a princess. Irving Berlin's musical, starring Ethel Merman, George Sanders and Donald O'Connor(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Bristol Cathedral. Antique experts James Lewis and Philip Serrell find objects to take to auction, including a bronze statue of Buddha(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball dishes more backstage gossip, talks to another one of the couples and has a sneak peek at a few of Saturday's costumes with designer Vicky Gill(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The Rev Richard Coles and actor Joe McFadden scour Scotland for antiques with David Harper and Philip Serrell, with a nailbiting auction ensuing after both teams buy near-identical items. Before the auction south of the border in Newcastle, Richard and David play football at one of Scotland's most famous clubs, while Joe and Philip end up going round in circles deep underground(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The six strongest chefs compete in the last quarter-final, inventing a dish that uses squash as the key ingredient. After the weakest two are sent home, the others cook two courses for three critics - Jay Rayner, Jimi Famurewa and Tracey MacLeod. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in Knockout Week(n)

programme.title: Giri/Haji
programme.desc: With Ian threatening to hand incriminating photos to the police, Kenzo and Sarah are divided in their motives as they each race to stop him. It looks as if Sarah will prevail but there are two things she did not anticipate - having to confront the mistakes of her past and Rodney having his own volatile plan for halting Ian. Meanwhile, Yuto is forced to take Angling hostage with the intention of killing him. But Kenzo cannot let that happen, and in order to stop his brother he must reveal his darkest secret(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig and Alan Davies are joined by Josh Widdicombe, Susan Calman and Matt Lucas for the long-running panel show - and this week it's a festive special, so Sandi's elves have been busy coming up with tough questions on the subject of - what else? - Noel. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers considered to be most interesting(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Reggie in China
programme.desc: Reggie Yates visits Beijing, and learns about the life of President Xi Jinping and his attitudes on the relationship between Communism and capitalism. He discovers the Chinese social media platforms that have sprung up in the absence of banned Western sites and talks to some of the city's wealthiest and most impoverished citizens(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef continues his 'journey without maps', leaving the main routes and tourist hotspots behind to embark on a personal road trip along picturesque and remote backroads. Here, he heads through France's eastern border regions of Alsace and Jura - famous for Comte cheese and home to his favourite French dish of choucroute garni. He then begins to head inland, visiting Burgundy for a memorable glass of Auxey-Duresses wine, and sampling oeuf en meurette with a difference. Following a memorable Sunday lunch near the French/Swiss border, he is inspired to recreate a traditional blueberry tart(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three talented artists travel to south Manchester where the mystery buyer's house is not what it seems - the owner has lost nearly all their possessions in a fire. The owner would like a piece of art to mark the end of a difficult journey, putting their home back together. Presented by Nick Knowles(n)

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: Twenty-five years since the Channel Tunnel was opened, many staff face retirement and new recruits are being drafted in. But the tests for wannabe shuttle drivers are ruthless, with a 90 per cent fail rate. Meanwhile, supercars turn up in convoy for the Le Mans 24-hour race. But as the French step up their passport controls, and the screens break down at terminal control, there are concerns that the vehicles will not make it to France in time(n)

programme.title: The A List
programme.desc: Harry makes a rash decision that puts him in Amber's crosshairs. Dev comes to a disturbing conclusion, and someone unexpected joins Amber's inner circle(n)

programme.title: The A List
programme.desc: Tensions with Amber reach breaking point - but while she sleeps, nightmares become reality and a shocking truth is revealed that changes everything. Thriller, with Ellie Duckles(n)

programme.title: The A List
programme.desc: Mia makes a final attempt to leave the island and escape Amber, who has more followers than ever. She turns to Luka for help, leading to heart-breaking consequences(n)

programme.title: The A List
programme.desc: As her allies start to fall, Mia goes to face Amber enemy head on, believing her own surrender will ensure her friends' escape. But Amber has one last surprise(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass investigate a notorious arsenic poisoning in which a distinguished lawyer and Army veteran hanged for the murder of his wife almost 100 years ago(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers get to work on a damaged bird brooch, a ragdoll who needs turning from shabby to shipshape and a trunk that has become mis-shapen after travelling the world(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Katy picks her four favourite letter writers to meet face-to-face at a speed dating event in London, including 74-year-old former professional footballer Carl, and painter and decorator John. Meanwhile, it's decision time for dairy farmers Martin and Grace, who have each invited three potential suitors back to their farms. Both are looking for a partner that will fall in love with not only them, but also their rural lifestyle and are hoping sparks will fly with at least one of their guests during their weekend stay(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Players answer questions to win visits to a sound-proof vault where they can see inside their opponents' cases. In a fast-paced endgame, they attempt to steal one another's cases via tense head-to-head challenges - but only the player who gets over the finish line first will win what's inside their case. Game show, hosted by Dan Walker(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Darcey Bussell's New Dance
programme.desc: The former ballerina profiles two modern dance groups that are taking familiar formats into uncharted territory via new works that fuse genres in ways that challenge the boundaries of the artform. Inspired by her own passion for boundary-defying routines, Darcey showcases the work of Boy Blue Entertainment, founded by choreographer Kendrick 'H20' Sandy and composer Michael Asante, whose Danny Boyle-directed street dance film Emancipation of Expressionism has found its way onto the GCSE Dance syllabus. She also turns attention to Canadian-based ice dancing collective Le Patin Libre, who use ice in daring new ways, encouraging audiences to view their format from a new perspective(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at television appearances made over the years by legendary Hollywood double act Ginger Rogers and Fred Astaire, capturing the milestones and highlights of their lives and careers. Narrated by veteran actress Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Top Hat (1935)
programme.desc: A woman on holiday in London meets a famous dancer staying in the same hotel when his enthusiastic rehearsing wakes her up. Attraction instantly develops between them, but she mistakenly believes he is a producer married to her friend. Musical, starring Fred Astaire, Ginger Rogers, Helen Broderick and Edward Everett Horton, with a score by Irving Berlin, including songs Cheek to Cheek and White Tie and Tails(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This time, Flog It! comes from the RAF Museum in Cosford, Shropshire. Antiques experts Adam Partridge and Claire Rawle are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Claire gets sentimental over a music box and Adam discovers a silver cigarette case from Russia. Paul Martin falls for an iconic bike and visits the nearby historic Ironbridge to investigate the start of the industrial revolution(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress ahead of Musicals Week. Celebrity super fans are in the studio to discuss all things ballroom(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Maldon & Tiptree v Newport County (Kick-off 7.55pm). Coverage of the second-round tie at the Breyer Group Stadium. Isthmian Division One North side Maldon have already produced one upset by claiming a 2-1 win over Leyton Orient in the first round to reach this stage for the first time in their history, and will now look to take the scalp of another League Two side. However, their professional opponents will see this as a good opportunity to reach the next round and potentially get drawn against a Premier League side(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Kerry Godliman, Milton Jones, Chris Washington and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Margate, Turner and the Prize
programme.desc: Since the painter JMW Turner came to Margate and marvelled at 'the loveliest skies in all Europe', newcomers have arrived, drawn to the seaside and also the sense that they might be able to find a new beginning in the Kent settlement. The documentary explores how these influxes of incomers - and the arrival of the Turner Institute for the 2019 Turner Prize - have impacted on the town. Contributors include Tracey Emin, who grew up in Margate, the Turner Prize shortlist artists and some of the local residents(n)

programme.title: Cosmopolis (2012)
programme.desc: A billionaire driven across Manhattan in a limousine discovers that an unexpected development in the stock market is poised to destroy his financial empire. As his fortune crumbles before his eyes, the real world starts to infringe on his sheltered existence through a series of strange encounters. David Cronenberg's drama, starring Robert Pattinson, Juliette Binoche and Paul Giamatti(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Canals - The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor tells the story of the people who operated the canal boats, carrying fuel and goods around the country. During the Victorian era they gained reputations for criminality, violence and drinking, but was such a reputation deserved? Liz discovers grisly canal crimes, investigates health and welfare on-board working boats, and looks at why canal children were last on the list to be offered safeguards and formal education. She reveals the campaigners who set out to tackle this injustice, including George Smith of Coalville, Leicestershire, and Sister Mary Ward of Stoke Bruerne, Northamptonshire(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders of Britain
programme.desc: Neil Oliver visits Bronze and Iron Age sites that were important to ancient Britons. At Flag Fen near Peterborough he investigates a vast causeway that has sacred objects placed among its timbers, while at Maiden Castle in Dorset he finds evidence that human sacrifices were conducted to ward off evil spirits. On Anglesey, he learns about the rituals and dark reputation of the druids and how the Romans viewed them as dangerous religious extremists, and at Lullingstone Roman Villa in Kent he examines wall paintings of pagan water deities(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In Northern Ireland, the graveyard of a Victorian workhouse sheds light on one of the most traumatic periods of modern Irish history, the Great Famine of 1845. Elsewhere, a team from Sheffield University want to understand the lives of people who occupied a village near the famous caves at Cresswell Crags in Nottinghamshire, and near Lincoln, a revisit to an Anglo Saxon site near proves rewarding(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: The return of the comedy series with Vic Reeves and Bob Mortimer, featuring sketches, slapstick, songs and stunts, accompanied by a series of special guests. This time, Judge Nutmeg dispenses punishment to an unsuspecting audience member, the Ghost Hunters are on the prowl, and Bob takes viewers back to the 1980s with a special performance(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they embark on an angling trip round the UK. The first stop is Norfolk, and while fishing for tench, Bob tries to impress Paul with his childhood fishing rod - and fails. They share nostalgia for their younger years and reveal how they recently came face to face with their own mortality due to heart disease(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for barbel in Hay-on-Wye, and sample the great outdoors in spectacular fashion when Mortimer decides to strip in celebration of their catch. Passing a graveyard, they muse about the future and chat to a local vicar about death and their own funerals. To lighten the mood, back in their wooden fishing cabin, Bob promises Paul a very special treat if they are successful with their angling efforts(n)

programme.title: Everyday Miracles: The Genius of Sofas, Stockings and Scanners
programme.desc: Professor Mark Miodownik's investigation into the complexities of modern invention concludes with an insight into how new materials enabled people to become curious about the world around them. He reveals the impact the advent of the bicycle had on the national gene pool, and highights how the concept of levitation provided the spiritually inclined to set out in search of their inner self(n)

programme.title: Utopia: In Search of the Dream
programme.desc: Professor Richard Clay asks whether utopian visions for living can ever reconcile the tension between the group and the individual, the rules and the desire to break free. In America he encounters experimental communities searching for greater meaning in life. Interviewees include architect Norman Foster and designer Shoji Sadao(n)

programme.title: From Scotland with Love
programme.desc: Documentary commissioned for the cultural festival accompanying the Commonwealth Games, in which Virginia Heath uses footage from the National Library of Scotland and the Scottish Screen Archive to explore themes of love, loss, resistance, migration, work and play. The film is set to a soundtrack composed by King Creosote, aka Fife singer-songwriter Kenny Anderson(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In Northern Ireland, the graveyard of a Victorian workhouse sheds light on one of the most traumatic periods of modern Irish history, the Great Famine of 1845. Elsewhere, a team from Sheffield University want to understand the lives of people who occupied a village near the famous caves at Cresswell Crags in Nottinghamshire, and near Lincoln, a revisit to an Anglo Saxon site near proves rewarding(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Canals - The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor examines the Manchester Ship Canal, hailed as the greatest engineering feat of the Victorian age. Widely regarded as troublesome outsiders who led a drunken life of ungodly debauchery, the navvies who built it toiled in a time of rising trade unionism, prompting Liz to consider how well they managed to organise themselves in campaigning for the best possible deal(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Solar System
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox reveals how a thin layer of gas can create some of the most wondrous sights in the solar system. Taking a ride in an English Electric Lightning aircraft, he flies up 18 kilometres to witness the darkness of space above the thin line of Earth's atmosphere. In the Namib desert, he tells the story of the planet Mercury and explains why he has chosen Saturn's moon Titan as his fourth wonder(n)

programme.title: Wild Weather with Richard Hammond
programme.desc: The Top Gear host reveals the hidden secrets at the heart of some of the world's most extreme weather in stunning detail, experiencing extraordinary phenomena first-hand. The first instalment focuses on wind, and sees the presenter brave temperatures of -50C on top of Mount Washington in New Hampshire, where in 1934 a wind speed of 231mph was recorded. Richard investigates how wind actually starts, walks into the centre of a man-made tornado and employs fire to make a 10m-high whirlwind(n)

programme.title: Elton John at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of interviews, news clips and archive BBC session performances from throughout the singer's career, including footage from The Old Grey Whistle Test and Top of the Pops. Songs featured include Saturday Night's Alright (For Fighting), I'm Still Standing and Circle of Life(n)

programme.title: Sings Disney Songs
programme.desc: Interpretations of music from the Walt Disney Studios' films over the past 40 years, featuring songs including the Bare Necessities, When You Wish Upon a Star and Zip-a-Dee-Doo-Dah. Celine Dion and Elton John are among the artists on clips from shows including Top of the Pops, Blue Peter, Shirley Bassey, It's Lulu, Louis Armstrong's Show of the Week and Brubeck(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Andrew Martin charts the evolution of British postage stamps and looks at how they became a national obsession, examining iconic examples and famous collectors, as well as meeting modern-day philatelists at a club(n)

programme.title: Utopia: In Search of the Dream
programme.desc: Professor Richard Clay asks whether utopia is a state of mind that can therefore be found within. He explores the many means people have created to immerse themselves in a perfect moment, of epiphany or transcendence, pushing the boundaries of artistic expression and pleasure. Seeking answers in a broad range of arts, the art historian tries to compose a haiku and uncovers traces of the hedonistic medieval carnival tradition in the churches and pubs of Lancashire. He also compares and contrasts different musical escapes, interviewing Acid House legend A Guy Called Gerald (aka Gerald Simpson) and the celebrated minimalist composer Steve Reich(n)

programme.title: Kill Your TV: Jim Moir's Weird World of Video Art
programme.desc: The comedian, aka Vic Reeves, explores video art, revealing how different generations hacked the tools of TV to pioneer new ways of creating work that could be beautiful, bewildering and experimental. He examines how this outsider art form became part of the creative establishment, as well as a purpose-built platform for a screen-obsessed world(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Solar System
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox reveals how a thin layer of gas can create some of the most wondrous sights in the solar system. Taking a ride in an English Electric Lightning aircraft, he flies up 18 kilometres to witness the darkness of space above the thin line of Earth's atmosphere. In the Namib desert, he tells the story of the planet Mercury and explains why he has chosen Saturn's moon Titan as his fourth wonder(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Susie Mathis present the edition first aired on November 17, featuring Yazz, All About Eve, INXS, Mica Paris, Tanita Tikaram, Robin Beck and Salt-N-Pepa(n)

programme.title: Sound of Song
programme.desc: Musician Neil Brand explores the elements that come together to make classic songs by recreating some of the most famous recording sessions in history. Among the samples analysed are Elvis Presley's slapback echo effect in Memphis, the Beatles' tape loops at Abbey Road, Phil Spector's 'wall of sound' technique, and the Beach Boys' pop symphonies. The presenter also looks at how an American soldier's discovery of magnetic tape in the ruins of Second World War Germany drove the emergence of the music studio and the rise of the producer as a new creative force for shaping the sound of song(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Andy Crane present the edition first aired on November 24, featuring performances by Tiffany, Bomb the Bass featuring Maureen, Deacon Blue, Michael Jackson, Pet Shop Boys, Iron Maiden, Bananarama, Chris de Burgh, Robin Beck and Hithouse(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: How country music adapted to the cultural changes of immediate post-war society between 1945 and 1953. Bill Monroe, Lester Flatt and Earl Scruggs transformed the traditional string band music into something more syncopated - bluegrass. And out of the bars and juke joints came honky-tonk, a new sound with electric guitars and songs about drinking, cheating and heartbreak. Contributors include Kris Kristofferson, Brenda Lee, Ricky Skaggs and Vince Gill. Narrated by Peter Coyote(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: The history of the American art form and how it evolved between 1953 and 1963 when the confluence of blues and hillbilly music at Sun Studios gave birth to rockabilly, the precursor of rock 'n' roll, with Elvis Presley and Johnny Cash at the forefront. In the recording studios of Music City, country music's twang was replaced by something smoother - the Nashville sound. Contributors include Loretta Lynn, Willie Nelson, Rosanne Cash, Elvis Costello, Brenda Lee and Merle Haggard. Narrated by Peter Coyote(n)

programme.title: Country Queens at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of archive performances by female country-music stars from a variety of BBC studio shows. Featured acts include Bobbie Gentry, Anne Murray, Emmylou Harris, Taylor Swift, Crystal Gayle, Tammy Wynette, Billie Jo Spears, and Lucinda Williams with Mary Chapin Carpenter(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team transforms a home in Bolton, Lancashire for the Taylor-Mann family, making the house more suitable for nine-year-old William, who has severe autism. Williams's condition means that he is extremely hypersensitive to noise, and being in close proximity to his siblings is often just too much for him to bear. Nick Knowles and the team, with the help of kind volunteers, transform the property to create a quiet, calm space that can help William feel as comfortable as possible(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates travel to the world-famous BBC Maida Vale Studios in West London, where Lord Sugar informs them that for their latest challenge, they are to become managers for musicians. The teams must audition unsigned artists before choosing which one to represent. They will then head to the studio to create a brand-new remix of one of their tracks, aiming to secure sales of the record at their own showcase events. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants is sent home from the competition(n)

programme.title: The Man Who Saw Too Much
programme.desc: At 106, Boris Pahor is the oldest living survivor of a Nazi concentration camp. He discusses life and death at Natzweiler, where almost half the prisoners died. Alan Yentob visits Pahor at his home in Trieste, where he talks about his fight against fascism and the Nazis(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, cameras in space show that the surface of the Earth is changing at a rapid pace as cities grow, forests disappear and glaciers melt. In ever growing grey of cities one man is feeding thousands of parakeets, while in Sumatra, a female orangutan and her daughter face life in a forest under threat. Elsewhere in Tanzania, local people use satellites to re-plant a forest, securing the future for a family of chimpanzees(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, hosted by Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ruby arrives to stay at the Vic with Mick and Ollie as part of the wife swap competition. Iqra and Adam are both unimpressed by their pairing with Adam booking a hotel to avoid staying with her. However, the pair reminisce and begin to repair their relationship. Things go from bad to worse for Martin as he clashes with Dotty again, leaving Bex disappointed. Ruby is shocked to hear that Martin and Stacey are over(n)

programme.title: Only Foals and Horses
programme.desc: Lisa Durham and Philippa Hughes as they battle to save the life of a thoroughbred with an infected leg. Lisa helps a local charity dedicated to rescuing abandoned ponies and Philippa wrestles with some feisty foals from the Gower marshes before they head off to the sales(n)

programme.title: The Wedding Dress Shop
programme.desc: Designer Samantha Buca gives a stern talking to police officer Claire, who cannot seem to find her confidence, while stressed-out Emma discovers she might have to find something else to wear for her wedding. Between appointments, Samm and her team discuss the big questions of life(n)

programme.title: Elton John: Uncensored
programme.desc: As he prepares for the publication of his autobiography, Me, and enjoys a break from his Farewell Yellow Brick Road tour, Elton John and Graham Norton chat about the musician's life and career, which spans more than 50 years. Elton discusses his childhood growing up as Reg Dwight in post-war Pinner, Middlesex, and looks back at his rise to global stardom in the 1970s. He also shares musical memories of the 80s and 90s, discusses his addictions, and how he managed to overcome them to become one of the world's most successful and prolific solo artists(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Swindon, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: A mix of light-hearted gossip and serious analysis about the process to leave the EU - and no doubt the forthcoming General Election - with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine the 1935 morphine poisoning of a care home resident Ada Baguley in Nottingham. Initially Ada was thought to have died from natural causes, but a post-mortem revealed her body to contain lethal doses of morphine. Dorothea Waddingham, the owner of the nursing home and primary care giver of Ada, was later convicted of murder(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks round a mid-terrace house in Carshalton, south London and a retail unit in Wallasey, Merseyside. Martin Roberts visits a dated flat in Pentre, south Wales(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: The fake online reviews ruining small businesses, as rivals pay others to trash their competitors. Plus, the counterfeit car parts that increase fuel costs and put lives at risk, and the vaccination scam that is jeopardising the lives of thousands of new-born puppies. Presented by Dominic Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley(n)

programme.title: A1: Britain's Longest Road
programme.desc: Police face a tough task to free a fragile driver. Traffic cops seize a car being driven with no insurance and no licence and a major crash forces the closure of the road(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Newbury Antiques and Collectors Fair in Berkshire, where experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams acquire items of value to take to auction. The host also pops across to Bristol to meet the maker of cigar box guitars(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: The launch night for Smears Without Fears arrives and Daniel's Christmas spirit loses its jingle. Ayesha's project helps a mother confront her anxiety and be there for her daughter(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max head out into wine country to investigate the killing of a dog at one of the island's most famous vineyards - Bodegas Negra. It transpires that the shooting is just one of an increasingly dramatic series of threats faced by property owner Hans Webber, his daughter Yvonne and son-in-law Rainer. Later, when Rainer's latest batch of white wine is dyed blue, all fingers point to arch-rival Emilio Byass(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple find a home in rural Worcestershire for their £450,000 budget so they can realise a dream of raising their family in the countryside. While in the region, Nicki joins the volunteers of the Severn Valley Railway, whose passion and dedication has helped keep this historic steam railway line running for over 50 years(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich set about transforming a sea of dingy gravel and paving for a couple eager for a peaceful haven. Owners Andrew and Dawn also want a design that allows the family caravan to be accommodated. Whose design will fall on stony ground?(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal expert Dom Chinea and wood restorer Will Kirk team up to tackle the restoration of a treasured steering wheel from a scrapped Scottish fishing boat. A worn-out Portsmouth football club mascot and a vintage cricket ball are also revived. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: BBC Election Debate
programme.desc: Nick Robinson presides over a seven-way podium debate between senior figures from the UK's major political parties, live from Cardiff. The format will see each of the panellists make short opening statements, which will then be followed by questions and debate. At the end of the debate the representatives will be invited to make short closing statements(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ruby unwillingly helps Ian with his councillor campaign, and coerces Martin into getting involved - but when Ian mentions Stacey, Martin shares some harsh words with his cousin. Mick realises Linda's drinking is getting out of control. Ash encourages Honey to have more confidence and helps her pick out a flattering outfit. Chantelle lays into Mitch about his parenting when he fails to return home after a date(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville creates a special blend of snuff, but will his customers notice any beneficial effects? The ladies get dressed up for line-dancing, while Gastric is prompted to go on a diet. A furtive Mr Newbold thinks he's found a pastime to share with Mrs Rossi - away from the prying eyes of Mrs Featherstone. However, a determined Black Widow enlists assistance from Granville and Leroy for a crash-course in how to ride a bicycle. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Peaky Blinders star Helen McCrory assumes the role of host, as contributors poke fun at the week's events. Stand-up comedian Chris McCausland is one of the panellists joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Guests include US politician Hillary Clinton and her daughter Chelsea, co-authors of The Book of Gutsy Women. Superstar chef and campaigner Jamie Oliver promotes his latest cookbook Veg, while comedian and actor David Mitchell talks about his collection of satirical columns, Dishonesty Is the Second-Best Policy. Plus, Kesha performs her current single Raising Hell(n)

programme.title: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: With a huge storm due to hit Wales, the marketing team comes up with the idea of sponsoring it as a publicity stunt. Meanwhile, Nez develops an accommodation app, but due to a combination of the bad weather, a technical mix-up and no alternative sleeping options, Elaine ends up using it and has to spend the night with him in his one-bedroom flat(n)

programme.desc: A report on the fake alcohol being served to British holiday makers in Greece. Plus, the children's toys that may contain gender altering chemicals(n)

programme.title: A1: Britain's Longest Road
programme.desc: An overturned lorry forces the closure of the road to Edinburgh. Meanwhile, emergency services battle to quell a devastating van fire which refuses to die down(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents as a team of gym enthusiasts take on a punk rock singer and an actress at Lincoln Antiques Fair, with help from experts Raj Bisram and Kate Bliss(n)

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ayesha and Bear try to convince Emma to lead their 'Smears Without Fears' campaign. Meanwhile, Daniel's therapy session forces him to confront an old demon(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max attempt to find a masked assailant who stole the famous Icon of St Nicholas, but the escape of a person of interest results in a furious Ines removing the pair from the case, causing a rift between the two detectives. When the icon suddenly reappears, Miranda and Max must put their differences aside as they are drawn into a furious cat-and-mouse chase(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A military couple seek a Shropshire home for their £500,000 budget, so Jonnie Irwin goes house hunting with the duo, and also meets a master bike frame builder(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are challenged to come up with a Mughal-inspired design for a tiny garden in Harrow(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life, including a collectable seesaw, a hand stitched bedspread and a gentlemen's shooting stick(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Interviews
programme.desc: The broadcaster interviews the leaders of the main parties in the run-up to the General Election(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team transforms a home in Bolton, Lancashire for the Taylor-Mann family, making the house more suitable for nine-year-old William, who has severe autism. Williams's condition means that he is extremely hypersensitive to noise, and being in close proximity to his siblings is often just too much for him to bear. Nick Knowles and the team, with the help of kind volunteers, transform the property to create a quiet, calm space that can help William feel as comfortable as possible(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates travel to the world-famous BBC Maida Vale Studios in West London, where Lord Sugar informs them that for their latest challenge, they are to become managers for musicians. The teams must audition unsigned artists before choosing which one to represent. They will then head to the studio to create a brand-new remix of one of their tracks, aiming to secure sales of the record at their own showcase events. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants is sent home from the competition(n)

programme.title: The Top Table
programme.desc: Young people come to the Top Table to face down our politicians in an exciting audience debate show, presented by Stephen Nolan(n)

programme.title: The Man Who Saw Too Much
programme.desc: At 106, Boris Pahor is the oldest living survivor of a Nazi concentration camp. He discusses life and death at Natzweiler, where almost half the prisoners died. Alan Yentob visits Pahor at his home in Trieste, where he talks about his fight against fascism and the Nazis(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, cameras in space show that the surface of the Earth is changing at a rapid pace as cities grow, forests disappear and glaciers melt. In ever growing grey of cities one man is feeding thousands of parakeets, while in Sumatra, a female orangutan and her daughter face life in a forest under threat. Elsewhere in Tanzania, local people use satellites to re-plant a forest, securing the future for a family of chimpanzees(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass investigate a notorious arsenic poisoning in which a distinguished lawyer and Army veteran hanged for the murder of his wife almost 100 years ago(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of a former pub in Sittingbourne in Kent, a mid-terrace in Creswell, Nottinghamshire and a terraced house in Fulham, west London(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Potentially lethal medicines being brought into the UK, the hairdryers that are more than just a rip-off and the vinyl records that do not come up to scratch(n)

programme.title: A1: Britain's Longest Road
programme.desc: The innocent party in a three-car collision ends up on the wrong side of the law. Police rescue two motorists caught short of fuel and a 300-ton load needs moving up the A1(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Weeks provide advice to two teams competing at the Royal Cornwall Showground near Wadebridge. Host Anita Manning meets a surfing legend in Newquay(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ayesha's project leads to squabbling, while a struggling Daniel lashes out and Al suspects Bear of hiding something. Zara helps a vulnerable widow reconnect with her husband's dog(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max investigate the disappearance of a supermodel, who apparently vanished into thin air when the train she was travelling on went through a tunnel. Elen Rhys stars(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple hoping to buy their first home together in Dorset on a budget of £800,000, and finds out about Dorset's ancient hedgerows(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in Oxton, Birkenhead, coming up with designs for musician Gareth, who says his garden is in need of a total rethink(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers get to work on a damaged bird brooch, a ragdoll who needs turning from shabby to shipshape and a trunk that has become mis-shapen after travelling the world(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, hosted by Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ruby arrives to stay at the Vic with Mick and Ollie as part of the wife swap competition. Iqra and Adam are both unimpressed by their pairing with Adam booking a hotel to avoid staying with her. However, the pair reminisce and begin to repair their relationship. Things go from bad to worse for Martin as he clashes with Dotty again, leaving Bex disappointed. Ruby is shocked to hear that Martin and Stacey are over(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: Summer arrives and with an increasing numbers of Brits holidaying at home, it looks like an ideal opportunity for Sainsbury's to cash in, especially in key Cornwall stores Newquay and Bude. However, Bristol depot manager Mike battles with traffic to deliver enough products for the holiday-makers, while in Penzance the fridges break down on one of the hottest days of the year. Meanwhile, at headquarters, product developer Charlotte is working on a vegan centrepiece for Christmas. Can she convince the press that her meat-free wellington is better than the competition?(n)

programme.title: Elton John: Uncensored
programme.desc: As he prepares for the publication of his autobiography, Me, and enjoys a break from his Farewell Yellow Brick Road tour, Elton John and Graham Norton chat about the musician's life and career, which spans more than 50 years. Elton discusses his childhood growing up as Reg Dwight in post-war Pinner, Middlesex, and looks back at his rise to global stardom in the 1970s. He also shares musical memories of the 80s and 90s, discusses his addictions, and how he managed to overcome them to become one of the world's most successful and prolific solo artists(n)

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Swindon, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: A mix of light-hearted gossip and serious analysis about the process to leave the EU - and no doubt the forthcoming General Election - with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine the 1935 morphine poisoning of a care home resident Ada Baguley in Nottingham. Initially Ada was thought to have died from natural causes, but a post-mortem revealed her body to contain lethal doses of morphine. Dorothea Waddingham, the owner of the nursing home and primary care giver of Ada, was later convicted of murder(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks round a mid-terrace house in Carshalton, south London and a retail unit in Wallasey, Merseyside. Martin Roberts visits a dated flat in Pentre, south Wales(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: The fake online reviews ruining small businesses, as rivals pay others to trash their competitors. Plus, the counterfeit car parts that increase fuel costs and put lives at risk, and the vaccination scam that is jeopardising the lives of thousands of new-born puppies. Presented by Dominic Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley(n)

programme.title: A1: Britain's Longest Road
programme.desc: Police face a tough task to free a fragile driver. Traffic cops seize a car being driven with no insurance and no licence and a major crash forces the closure of the road(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Newbury Antiques and Collectors Fair in Berkshire, where experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams acquire items of value to take to auction. The host also pops across to Bristol to meet the maker of cigar box guitars(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: The launch night for Smears Without Fears arrives and Daniel's Christmas spirit loses its jingle. Ayesha's project helps a mother confront her anxiety and be there for her daughter(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max head out into wine country to investigate the killing of a dog at one of the island's most famous vineyards - Bodegas Negra. It transpires that the shooting is just one of an increasingly dramatic series of threats faced by property owner Hans Webber, his daughter Yvonne and son-in-law Rainer. Later, when Rainer's latest batch of white wine is dyed blue, all fingers point to arch-rival Emilio Byass(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple find a home in rural Worcestershire for their £450,000 budget so they can realise a dream of raising their family in the countryside. While in the region, Nicki joins the volunteers of the Severn Valley Railway, whose passion and dedication has helped keep this historic steam railway line running for over 50 years(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich set about transforming a sea of dingy gravel and paving for a couple eager for a peaceful haven. Owners Andrew and Dawn also want a design that allows the family caravan to be accommodated. Whose design will fall on stony ground?(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal expert Dom Chinea and wood restorer Will Kirk team up to tackle the restoration of a treasured steering wheel from a scrapped Scottish fishing boat. A worn-out Portsmouth football club mascot and a vintage cricket ball are also revived. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.desc: Nick Robinson presides over a seven-way podium debate between senior figures from the UK's major political parties, live from Cardiff. The format will see each of the panellists make short opening statements, which will then be followed by questions and debate. At the end of the debate the representatives will be invited to make short closing statements(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ruby unwillingly helps Ian with his councillor campaign, and coerces Martin into getting involved - but when Ian mentions Stacey, Martin shares some harsh words with his cousin. Mick realises Linda's drinking is getting out of control. Ash encourages Honey to have more confidence and helps her pick out a flattering outfit. Chantelle lays into Mitch about his parenting when he fails to return home after a date(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville creates a special blend of snuff, but will his customers notice any beneficial effects? The ladies get dressed up for line-dancing, while Gastric is prompted to go on a diet. A furtive Mr Newbold thinks he's found a pastime to share with Mrs Rossi - away from the prying eyes of Mrs Featherstone. However, a determined Black Widow enlists assistance from Granville and Leroy for a crash-course in how to ride a bicycle. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You

programme.title: The Blame Game

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Guests include US politician Hillary Clinton and her daughter Chelsea, co-authors of The Book of Gutsy Women. Plus, Kesha performs her current single Raising Hell(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home(n)

programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. programme.desc: Nature documentary revealing how animals cope with the demands of raising their offspring. The first episode follows newborn lambs and foals as their mothers help them to learn to walk, a gorilla in a safari park going through a difficult birth and a white rhino mutilated by hunters that gives birth to a calf, but will have a hard time protecting it. Plus, a look at how highland cows and pigs nurse their young - and a seal that is not able to. Featuring Adam Henson, narrated by Hugh Bonneville(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: For the next leg of his journey, Gino D'Acampo boards an old steam train run by ex-railroad men and volunteers which takes him through the beautiful Tuscan countryside. His first stop is the town of Asciano, where locals try to compete with Siena's famous Palio horse race - only here they ride donkeys. Then it's on to the Chianti region where he visits a vineyard where they don't only make wine, but they also bathe in it too, an ancient tradition dating back to Roman times. After a luxurious wine bath, Gino cooks up a dish fit for any Emperor - farfalle pasta with funghi and spinach(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: For the next leg of his journey, Gino D'Acampo boards an old steam train run by ex-railroad men and volunteers which takes him through the beautiful Tuscan countryside. His first stop is the town of Asciano, where locals try to compete with Siena's famous Palio horse race - only here they ride donkeys. Then it's on to the Chianti region where he visits a vineyard where they don't only make wine, but they also bathe in it too, an ancient tradition dating back to Roman times. After a luxurious wine bath, Gino cooks up a dish fit for any Emperor - farfalle pasta with funghi and spinach(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Piers meets Caitlyn Jenner, who for the first 65 years of her life was known as Bruce, a former Olympic gold medal-winning decathlete. Looking back at her remarkable life, she opens up about her feud with her ex-wife and stepchildren in the Kardashian family, reflects on winning Olympic gold while simultaneously battling gender issues and discusses the death of her younger brother(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home(n)

Meanwhile an unexpected setback leaves Det Supt Dave Kelly with a tough decision to make. programme.title: Inspector Morse

programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The GP is left holding the baby after upsetting the new childminder, but luckily Mike agrees to look after James at short notice. However, Louisa is not happy with the arrangement and an argument erupts between the newlyweds just as their guests, Dennis and Karen Dodds, arrive for dinner - and that's only the first of the evening's problems. Meanwhile, Ruth is roped into taking part in a phone-in programme on Radio Portwenn and unwittingly answers a call from PC Penhale, who wants to know how to make friends in the village(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a pub landlady is crushed to death by a giant box during a magic show organised by famous illusionist Gideon Latimer, pathologist Kate discovers the cables had been deliberately sabotaged. Evangelising curate Andrew Maplin claims the tragedy is God's vengeance for the pagan traditions still held deep in Midsomer Oaks, but Barnaby and Nelson fear Gideon was the real target. Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee and Tamzin Malleson star in the murder mystery, with Joe Absolom, Andrew-Lee Potts, Justin Salinger, Amanda Burton and Stephanie Leonidas(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Jonas Armstrong plays a Territorial Army soldier sent home to his family from Afghanistan after suffering serious facial injuries. Though back among people who love him, he faces a struggle to come to terms with the terrible physical and psychological scars from his tour - and to reconcile his actions in the split seconds that changed his life. With Ian Puleston-Davies and Siobhan Finneran(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah Louise collapses in agony, and Martin looks after her when she undergoes emergency operation on her appendix. Curly decides to employ Vera when he sees how upset she is(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah comes out of intensive care. Curly is stunned when Vera stands in for Ivy at Bettabuy. Percy advises Maud to stop fighting Maureen as she needs her(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Archie has a disappointment with his pantomime. There's a doctor's family in the house while at Whiteley's farm. A secret meeting leads to a dramatic decision(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Beckindale's newest arrivals settle in. Nick is shattered to be betrayed. Elizabeth Pollard finds herself hurling much more than insults(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Blakey cuts the canteen's opening hours, but when he sacks one of the staff, things really turn nasty. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.desc: Stan and Jack eavesdrop on Blakey's medical - and completely misinterpret its findings. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo are entranced when they meet Robin's older brother. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Mrs Tishell decides she can no longer hide her love for Martin and, fuelled by a cocktail of drugs, takes drastic action. While James Henry is in her care, she boards a bus out of Portwenn and leaves a message for Dr Ellingham to 'meet them at the castle', forcing Martin, Louisa, Ruth and PC Penhale to mount a desperate search for the pair. Starring Martin Clunes, Selina Cadell and Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A comic convention is held in Carver Valley, but the event gets off to a sinister start when the latest creation of a mysterious writer is stolen from the organiser's house and burned. Barnaby takes up the case, which turns murderous when a former supermodel is killed during the event. The only clue is an unusual issue of the missing comics, which seems to reveal the dark secrets of six local people - and provides the police with six likely suspects. Guest starring Bill Bailey and Jemma Redgrave(n)

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: When potential witness Claire Olssen is intimidated into changing her statement, the investigation team look for alternative ways to reinforce their case. As Steve returns to work, Kelly is offered a deal that tests his resolve(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Downtrodden detective Jack Frost investigates after an unidentified man's body is found on top of a pile of glass at a council recycling centre - and the subsequent autopsy reveals a few surprises. Another case is soon on the cards when an apparent suicide raises suspicions. On the personal front, Frost decides to get in shape and joins a gym, where he falls for the charms of his fitness trainer. Starring David Jason and Claire Cox(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Portwenn is buzzing with excitement as the final preparations are made for the Doc and Louisa's big day. However, Martin is irritated by PC Penhale, who wrongly presumes he is best man - and to make matters worse, the bride is late. This isn't the first time the couple have tried to get married - will the villagers have to put the confetti back in the box again? Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz star, with John Marquez, Eileen Atkins and Ian McNeice(n)

programme.desc: At the Luxton Deeping Crime Festival, the unveiling of a newly discovered novel by deceased Midsomer crime writer George Summersbee is jeopardised when the manuscript is stolen and local artist Suzie Colebrooke is electrocuted by a booby-trapped roulette wheel. Can new father Barnaby untangle a web of jealousy and obsession to find the killer? With Fiona Dolman, Tamzin Malleson, Liberty Mills, Una Stubbs and Georgia Taylor(n)

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: Mercer and his associates are tried for their roles in the killing of 11-year-old Rhys Jones, but the verdict fails to bring closure for the boy's parents Melanie and Steve as the fractures in their relationship come to a head. Meanwhile an unexpected setback leaves Det Supt Dave Kelly with a tough decision to make. The concluding part of the fact-based drama centred on the devastating impact of a child's murder amid a wave of gang violence in Liverpool, starring Stephen Graham, Sinead Keenan and Brían F O'Byrne(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Another chance to see John Thaw's farewell appearance as Colin Dexter's Oxford detective. Kevin Whately co-stars, with James Grout, Jesse Birdsall, Clare Holman and Paul Freeman(n)

programme.desc: Lynn's house is still on fire. Alan puts together a missing persons list. Shirley arranges a base for the emergency services to work from(n)

programme.desc: Percy is stunned when Emily announces she is going to marry Bernard. Tricia is furious when Curly stands her up. Des is annoyed when Alex tells him he is sending him to Newcastle(n)

programme.desc: Mrs Baker calls on Gail and breaks down. Reg is furious when Ken tells him he is not interested in buying the flat. Derek organises a birthday party for Mavis(n)

programme.desc: Arthur is conned into buying a dodgy greyhound and lumbered with a pile of knocked-off videos, and it is up to Terry to save him from a fate worse than death. Vintage drama, starring Dennis Waterman, George Cole and Patrick Malahide(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad trails diamond thief Jimmy Fleet on the inside, with Carter acting undercover as a fellow prisoner aiming to gain his confidence.
